exam_title: CAPM Fellowship Final Exam (Remote Mastery Verification)

anchors.0: Incorrect, unsafe, or absent. Critical misunderstanding or refusal to engage.
anchors.1: Major gaps. Fellowship content not meaningfully understood or demonstrated.
anchors.2: Partially demonstrated. Some understanding but falls short of fellowship-level mastery.
anchors.3: Adequate mastery. Meets minimum competency expected of a fellowship graduate.
anchors.4: Strong mastery. Nuanced, goes beyond minimum with clinically meaningful integration.
anchors.5: Excellent mastery. Exceptional depth, integration, and clinical judgment.

procedure.minimum_competency_definition: A minimally competent CAPM fellow has completed all fellowship requirements and demonstrates the lowest acceptable level of knowledge and clinical reasoning to practice independently as a CAPM diplomate. They have no critical safety gaps and no major conceptual errors, but may lack depth or nuance beyond the essential minimum.
procedure.rating_question: What score (0–5) would a minimally competent CAPM fellow receive on this domain in this section of the exam?
rating_instructions: Rate the candidate who is just barely qualified to pass — not the average or excellent fellow.
A rating of 3 indicates the candidate meets minimum competency on that domain.
Ratings below 3 indicate below-minimum performance on that domain.
You may rate in 0.5 increments. Provide a rationale if your rating is unusually high or low.
After Round 1, items with panelist SD > 1.0 will be flagged for group discussion before Round 2.

cut_score_formula.description: Weighted sum of section-level minimum competency percentages.
steps: For each section: sum the mean panelist ratings across all 6 domains = mce_raw (0–30)
Apply blueprint weight: section_contribution = mce_pct × blueprint_weight

panelist_qualifications: Current or former CAPM fellow in good standing
Active clinical practice in companion animal pain management
No financial conflict of interest with exam pass rates
Commitment to 2-round rating process (approximately 3 hours total)
